reasoning: The document contains a critical programmatic defect due to header/footer elements being retained in the output across multiple pages, which creates duplicate artifacts for screen readers. Additionally, there are several major defects including displaced and non-hyperlinked URLs, broken list hierarchy, and potentially missing images. Because of the critical defect, the document must be rejected.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (3): State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries
3441 ; Proclamation PT-2-2024
Pg. 2; Proclamation PT-2-2024
Pg. 3
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=3, pages_affected=3/3. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

The source document contains a continuous numbered list using Roman numerals (I through X). While the output correctly identifies items I through VI as a list on Page 1, it incorrectly converts the subsequent Roman numeral list items on Pages 2 and 3 into headings. This completely breaks the list hierarchy, as items VII, VIII, IX, and X are structurally equivalent to items I through VI in the source PDF but are rendered as unassociated H2 and H3 elements in the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
VII. PENDER COUNTY
A. That area 100 feet on either side...

VIII. NEW HANOVER COUNTY
A. Mason Channel - from the IWW...

concerns.description: Link text pulled over but not hyperlinked
concerns.analysis: Several clickable inline hyperlinks in the source document were extracted as plain flat text without markdown link syntax. As a result, the inline text is no longer hyperlinked in the main flow of the text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Item II: '...visit the NCDMF Interactive Map.' | Item X.E: 'Go to the latest proclamation...' | Item X.H: 'See proclamation PT-1-2024...'
concerns.output_quote: "text": "II. Designated primary nursery areas... visit the NCDMF Interactive Map." | "text": "E. Hatched areas on the attached maps indicate... Go to the latest proclamation for areas..." | "text": "H. See proclamation PT-1-2024 for details..."
concerns.location: Page 1, List item II | Page 3, List item E | Page 3, List item H

concerns.analysis:
[Downgraded: image_count_ratio=1.00, placeholder_ratio=0.00 contradict LLM claim] Examined the first page of the source PDF, which contains a meaningful agency seal (North Carolina Marine Fisheries) in the top left corner. In the HTML output, this image is missing its proper `` element and is instead rendered as a plain text placeholder bracketed by `

[Image: ...]

`. This fails the first-page logo requirement.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Top left corner of page 1 shows the circular North Carolina Marine Fisheries seal/logo.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries featuring a fish over an outline of the state]

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 4 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=4, examples=['https://ncdenr.maps.arcgis.com/home/webmap/viewer.html?useEx', 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/marine-fisheries/rules-pr', 'https://files.nc.gov/deq/documents/2023-12/PT-1-2024%20Amend']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

The source document makes extensive use of underlined text to emphasize section titles and location names within list items (e.g., 'CARTERET COUNTY/ONSLOW COUNTY', 'West Channel - Island Channel'). This underline emphasis is completely missing from the output text.

action_items: Ensure header/footer regions are properly excluded from the extraction
Correct list hierarchy for Roman numerals across pages
Fix hyperlink extraction to keep links inline and properly formatted
Ensure the agency seal image is properly rendered with an tag
